articles.title: Welcome to BeastlyFacts - Why We're Here
articles.excerpt: Welcome to BeastlyFacts! Honest animal care guides, fun facts, and practical advice for reptile and small mammal owners.

articles.title: Axolotl Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Axolotls look deceptively simple to keep, a tank of water and a strange, smiling salamander. The real cost driver isn't obvious until you get into the specifics: keeping the water cold enough is what actually determines your budget.

articles.title: Axolotl Handling: What to Know
articles.excerpt: Axolotls are not a hands-on pet, and this isn't a preference, it's about their physical vulnerability. Handling should be minimal and reserved for genuine necessity, not casual interaction.

articles.title: Axolotl Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Axolotls are hardy in the right conditions and surprisingly fragile outside them. A large share of what goes wrong traces back to water that's too warm, which makes temperature control the single most important thing to get right.

articles.title: Axolotl Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Axolotl husbandry comes down to a short list of rules that matter a lot: keep the water cold, skip the gravel, and don't overthink the rest. Get those two things right and this is a genuinely manageable pet.

articles.title: Ball Python Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Ball pythons have one of the best cost-to-lifespan ratios of any commonly kept reptile: $300-800 to set up, then genuinely low costs for the 20-30 years after.

articles.title: Ball Python Feeding: Everything You Need to Know
articles.excerpt: Ball pythons are legendary for refusing food. Before you panic, here's what's actually normal, and when to actually worry.

articles.title: How to Handle a Ball Python Correctly
articles.excerpt: Ball pythons are defensive as babies and mellow as adults, and most of what makes handling go well isn't grip or technique, it's timing.

articles.title: Ball Python Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Ball pythons are sensitive to husbandry mistakes in specific, predictable ways. Most of what shows up here traces back to temperature, humidity, or hygiene.

articles.title: Is a Ball Python Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: The federal python ban that shows up in Everglades news coverage never included ball pythons. They're legal almost everywhere, with two real exceptions: Hawaii's total snake ban, and New York City's ban on the entire python family.

articles.title: Ball Python Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Ball python husbandry has shifted toward bigger, more furnished enclosures. Here's what an adult setup actually needs, and why humidity deserves more attention than it gets.

articles.title: Ball Python vs. Corn Snake: Beginner Snake Guide
articles.excerpt: Ball python or corn snake? The real differences in feeding reliability, humidity needs, escape risk, and lifespan commitment - not just which one looks calmer at the pet store.

articles.title: Bearded Dragon Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: What a bearded dragon actually costs: $400-800 to set up right, $50-108 a month after that, and the vet bills correct husbandry prevents.

articles.title: How to Handle a Bearded Dragon Correctly
articles.excerpt: Bearded dragons are one of the most handleable pet lizards, but getting the timing, grip, and body language right early is what builds a dragon that actually enjoys it.

articles.title: Bearded Dragon Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Most serious bearded dragon health problems trace back to husbandry, not bad luck. Here's what to watch for, from MBD to yellow fungus disease, and when to call a vet.

articles.title: Bearded Dragon Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Bearded dragon husbandry has moved on from what old care sheets say. Bigger enclosures, stronger UVB, and more careful temperature gradients are now the standard.

articles.title: Bearded Dragon vs. Leopard Gecko: Which to Get?
articles.excerpt: Both get recommended as the best beginner reptile. Here's the honest difference in cost, daily care, and commitment before you pick one.

articles.title: Betta Fish Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Betta fish have a reputation as a starter pet you can keep in almost nothing, which is exactly the misconception worth correcting. A proper setup costs more than the bowl-and-fish combo suggests, but it's still one of the more affordable pets you can keep once it's built.

articles.title: How to Handle a Betta Fish Safely
articles.excerpt: Betta fish are not a hands-on pet in the way a guinea pig or a bearded dragon is. The honest answer to how you handle a betta is almost always: you don't, at least not with bare hands.

articles.title: Betta Fish Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Almost every common betta illness traces back to one of two things: water quality or water temperature. That's genuinely good news, since it means the first response to a sick betta is almost always the same starting point.

articles.title: Betta Fish Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: The bowl-on-a-desk image most people have of betta fish is outdated. Modern betta care centers on a proper heated, filtered tank, and the setup itself isn't complicated once you know what actually matters.

articles.title: Betta Fish vs. Goldfish: Beginner Fish Guide
articles.excerpt: Betta or goldfish? The real differences in tank size, temperature needs, social behavior, and lifespan - not just which one is cheaper at the pet store.

articles.title: Betta Fish Water Parameters Guide
articles.excerpt: The betta water numbers that matter - temperature, pH, ammonia, nitrite, and nitrate targets - plus what to test, how often, and which are non-negotiable.

articles.title: Bioactive Setups for Bearded Dragons
articles.excerpt: A complete guide to building a self-sustaining bioactive terrarium for bearded dragons with live plants and a cleanup crew.

articles.title: Is a Box Turtle Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: Taking a box turtle from the wild is restricted in most states, but even a legally acquired, captive-bred one isn't automatically unrestricted. Florida caps possession at two per person, and a handful of states attach real conditions.

articles.title: Why Is My Budgie's Cere Changing Color?
articles.excerpt: What your budgie's cere color says about sex, age, and health - why a female's cere turns brown in breeding condition, and when a change means trouble.

articles.title: Budgie Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Budgies are one of the more affordable pet birds to buy, but avian vet care is scarcer and pricier than dog or cat care, which is the piece people most often miss.

articles.title: How to Tame and Handle a Budgie
articles.excerpt: How you approach handling a budgie depends a lot on where it came from. A hand-raised bird may step up on day one; an untamed one needs a slower process that rewards patience.

articles.title: Budgie Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Birds are prey animals that hide illness as a survival instinct. By the time a budgie looks visibly sick, it's often further along than a mammal would be at the same stage.

articles.title: Budgie Cage Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Budgies are small, but their housing needs get underestimated more than almost any other part of their care. A few specific details matter more than overall size alone.

articles.title: Budgie vs. Cockatiel: Beginner Bird Guide
articles.excerpt: Budgie or cockatiel? The real differences in noise, lifespan, talking ability, and how affectionate each one actually is - not just which one is smaller at the pet store.

articles.title: Cat Hairballs vs Vomiting: When to Worry
articles.excerpt: An occasional hairball is normal. Frequent vomiting isn't - here's how to tell the difference and what's actually driving it.

articles.title: Chameleon Hydration: Drippers vs. Misters
articles.excerpt: Drippers, misters, and foggers compared for chameleon hydration - how each works, which fits your setup, and how to spot dehydration before it's serious.

articles.title: Chinchilla Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Chinchillas are a genuine long-term commitment, both in years and in the specialized setup they need. The animal itself isn't the expensive part, the cage, the exercise wheel, and keeping the room the right temperature are where the real budget goes.

articles.title: How to Handle a Chinchilla Safely
articles.excerpt: Chinchillas are a genuine handling pet, unlike some of the more hands-off species out there, but they come with two specific physical quirks worth understanding before you pick one up for the first time.

articles.title: Chinchilla Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Chinchillas hide illness well and can decline quickly once something is genuinely wrong, which makes knowing the warning signs, and one specific environmental risk, especially important with this species.

articles.title: Is a Chinchilla Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: We couldn't find a single US state that actually bans pet chinchillas, despite blog posts claiming California and Texas do. The real legal story is a wild-population treaty that explicitly exempts domesticated pets.

articles.title: Chinchilla Cage & Housing Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Chinchilla care has a few rules that genuinely surprise new owners, especially around bathing. Get the cage, the temperature, and the dust bath right, and the rest of this species' care is fairly manageable.

articles.title: Cockatiel Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Cockatiels are relatively affordable to buy, but the real budgeting story here is how long you'll be caring for one. A 15 to 20 year lifespan means this is a long-term financial commitment even though the entry cost is modest.

articles.title: How to Tame and Handle a Cockatiel
articles.excerpt: Taming a cockatiel is a trust-building process more than a technique, and it goes faster when you let the bird set the pace rather than rushing toward a step-up cue on day one.

articles.title: Cockatiel Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Birds hide illness as a survival instinct, which means a cockatiel that looks visibly sick is often further along than the same symptoms would suggest in a cat or dog.

articles.title: Cockatiel Cage Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: A cockatiel's cage is the single biggest factor in its day-to-day wellbeing, and a few specific details, width, bar spacing, and placement, matter more than overall size alone.

articles.title: Why Cockatoos Scream and Pluck Feathers
articles.excerpt: Why cockatoos scream and pluck their feathers - the psychology behind these behaviors, what genuinely reduces them, and what makes them worse.

articles.title: Corn Snake Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Corn snakes are one of the most budget-friendly beginner snakes: $250-600 for a solid setup, then genuinely low costs for the two decades after.

articles.title: How to Handle a Corn Snake Correctly
articles.excerpt: Corn snakes are docile and hardy as adults, but juveniles can be fast, wiggly, and genuinely defensive before they settle into the calmer temperament the species is known for.

articles.title: Corn Snake Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Corn snakes are one of the hardier pet snakes, and nearly every health problem they develop traces back to husbandry rather than bad luck.

articles.title: Corn Snake Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Corn snakes are a genuinely forgiving species to set up correctly, with one non-negotiable exception: whatever heat source you choose, it needs a thermostat.

articles.title: Crested Gecko Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Crested geckos sit near the affordable end of the reptile hobby, and unlike heat-hungry desert species, most of the ongoing cost is food and humidity, not electricity.

articles.title: How to Handle a Crested Gecko Correctly
articles.excerpt: Crested geckos are gentle and easy to handle once settled, but unlike leopard geckos, a dropped tail never grows back, which changes how carefully you should handle one.

articles.title: Crested Gecko Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Nearly every crested gecko health problem traces back to calcium, humidity, and substrate choices. Get those right and most of this list never shows up.

articles.title: Why Humidity Is Everything for Crested Geckos
articles.excerpt: Crested geckos need precise humidity to shed cleanly, stay hydrated, and thrive. Get it wrong and problems stack fast - here's exactly how to nail it.

articles.title: Crested Gecko Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Crested geckos flip the usual reptile setup logic. Where most lizards need a hot basking spot, this species needs you to actively avoid overheating them.

articles.title: Dog Bloat (GDV): Warning Signs to Know
articles.excerpt: Bloat can kill a healthy dog within hours - the early signs, which breeds are most at risk, and why this is always a drive-to-the-vet-now emergency.

articles.title: Ferret Adrenal Disease: Signs to Know
articles.excerpt: How to spot the telltale hair-loss pattern of ferret adrenal disease, why early spay and neuter age matters, and the treatment options that actually help.

articles.title: Is a Ferret Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: Ferrets are legal pets almost everywhere in the US, with two real exceptions: California and Hawaii ban them outright, and New York City bans them locally even though the rest of New York State allows them.

articles.title: Is a Giant African Millipede Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: There was never a 2006 blanket import ban on giant African millipedes, despite what hobbyist forums repeat. The real rule is a standing USDA import permit, plus real state variation from Florida's named permit to Oregon's outright approval.

articles.title: Goldfish Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Goldfish have the worst reputation-to-reality gap of almost any common pet. The fish itself costs next to nothing, but proper goldfish keeping requires a genuinely large tank and serious filtration, exactly the part the classic fish-in-a-bowl image leaves out entirely.

articles.title: How to Handle a Goldfish Safely
articles.excerpt: Like most fish, goldfish should not be routinely picked up by hand. This isn't an overcautious rule, their protective slime coat is a genuine, physical barrier against disease, and bare hands remove it on contact.

articles.title: Goldfish Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Goldfish are hardier than their reputation suggests when kept in the right conditions, but a handful of issues come up often enough to be worth knowing before they show up in your own tank.

articles.title: Goldfish Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: If you take away one thing from goldfish care, make it this: the bowl is wrong, and the tank you actually need is bigger than most people expect for what looks like a small, simple fish.

articles.title: The Goldfish Bowl Myth Debunked
articles.excerpt: Why the goldfish bowl and 3-second-memory myths are wrong, what undersized tanks really do to goldfish, and the true space requirements by variety.

articles.title: Guinea Pig Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Guinea pigs are cheap to adopt and expensive to underestimate. The animal itself costs almost nothing, but guinea pigs are social animals that need a same-species companion, so most of the real budgeting math here is really about caring for two, not one.

articles.title: How to Handle a Guinea Pig Safely
articles.excerpt: Guinea pigs are prey animals, and being lifted off the ground is, to their instincts, exactly what a predator attack looks like. That doesn't mean they can't become comfortable with handling, it means the approach matters more than it would with a less nervous pet.

articles.title: Guinea Pig Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Guinea pigs mask illness well, which means routine care matters more than it might seem to when everything looks fine. A handful of conditions come up often enough that every owner should be able to recognize them.

articles.title: Guinea Pig Scurvy: Vitamin C Guide
articles.excerpt: Guinea pigs can't make their own vitamin C, unlike almost every other small pet. Here's what scurvy actually looks like and why pellets alone aren't enough.

articles.title: Guinea Pig Cage Setup & Diet Guide
articles.excerpt: Guinea pig care is fairly forgiving once the basics are right, but there's one dietary detail that genuinely sets this species apart from almost every other small pet, and getting it wrong has real health consequences.

articles.title: Hamster vs. Guinea Pig: Which Is Right for You?
articles.excerpt: Hamster or guinea pig? The honest differences in space, social needs, lifespan, and daily care - not just which one looks cuter in the store.

articles.title: Hermit Crab Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Hermit crabs are often sold as an impulse buy at beach shops and boardwalk stands, which is exactly the wrong way to think about them. Done properly, they need a real setup, and they're social animals that shouldn't be kept alone.

articles.title: How to Handle a Hermit Crab Safely
articles.excerpt: Hermit crabs are prey animals, and they're not really a pet built for regular handling the way a guinea pig or a chinchilla is. Minimal, gentle, purposeful handling is the right approach here, not casual daily interaction.

articles.title: Hermit Crab Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Here's the thing to understand upfront about hermit crab health: there's essentially no practical veterinary care available for this species, similar to the situation with pet tarantulas. That reshapes everything about how to approach their wellbeing, husbandry and prevention do nearly all of the work.

articles.title: Hermit Crab Enclosure Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Hermit crab care is genuinely different from most small-pet setups, this is a species that needs both land and water access, and its most important requirement, humidity, is the one first-time owners most often get wrong.

articles.title: Is a Hognose Snake Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: Hognose snakes are mildly rear-fanged, but that doesn't make them a regulated venomous species anywhere we checked. The real restriction to watch for is Georgia, which bans keeping native hognose as pets regardless of captive-bred origin.

articles.title: Invertebrate Molting 101: What's Normal
articles.excerpt: How molting works in tarantulas, hermit crabs, and jumping spiders - normal signs versus a failed molt, and why you should never disturb a molting pet.

articles.title: Leopard Gecko Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Leopard geckos are one of the more budget-friendly reptiles to keep: roughly $250-400 to set up, then $20-50 a month after that.

articles.title: How to Handle a Leopard Gecko Correctly
articles.excerpt: Leopard geckos tolerate handling well once settled, but their single biggest physical vulnerability, tail loss, is the one thing every owner needs to understand first.

articles.title: Leopard Gecko Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Leopard geckos are generally hardy, but stick tail disease has no cure and is easy to miss early. Here's what every owner should know how to recognize.

articles.title: Leopard Gecko Tank Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Leopard geckos have a reputation for being low-maintenance, and mostly they are, but a few details around humidity and heat placement matter more than the simplicity suggests.

articles.title: The Complete Leopard Gecko Temperature Guide
articles.excerpt: Leopard gecko temperatures made simple: warm hide 88-92°F, cool side 70-77°F, the right heating methods, and the common mistakes that cause illness.

articles.title: Leopard Gecko vs. Crested Gecko: Beginner Guide
articles.excerpt: Leopard gecko or crested gecko? The real differences in diet, enclosure shape, heating needs, and tail regrowth - not just which one looks friendlier at the pet store.

articles.title: Praying Mantis Ootheca: What to Do
articles.excerpt: What a praying mantis ootheca is, whether it can be fertile without a male, how to care for it, and what to do when dozens of nymphs hatch at once.

articles.title: Rabbit Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Rabbits have a reputation as a simple starter pet, but the real cost picture is bigger than the low adoption fee suggests, especially once spay/neuter is factored in.

articles.title: Rabbit GI Stasis: A Silent Emergency
articles.excerpt: Rabbit GI stasis can turn fatal within hours - the early warning signs, why unlimited hay matters so much, and when it becomes an emergency vet visit.

articles.title: How to Handle a Rabbit Safely
articles.excerpt: Rabbits are more physically fragile than most first-time owners expect. An unsupported or panicked rabbit can genuinely injure itself trying to escape your grip.

articles.title: Rabbit Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Rabbits need a rabbit-savvy exotic vet, not a standard small-animal clinic. Here's what to watch for, from GI stasis to dental disease, and when it's a true emergency.

articles.title: Rabbit Housing Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Rabbit housing guidance has shifted substantially. The space rabbits actually need is much larger than what's traditionally been sold as a starter cage.

articles.title: Is a Red-Eared Slider Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: There's no federal ban on owning a red-eared slider, only a 1975 rule against selling tiny ones. The real restrictions come from state invasive-species laws, and Colorado just added a big one with a deadline one week away.

articles.title: Understanding Reptile Shedding (Ecdysis)
articles.excerpt: Shedding is one of the most misunderstood aspects of reptile care. Here's what's actually happening, what's normal, and when you need to step in.

articles.title: Brumation in Pet Snakes Explained
articles.excerpt: What brumation is, why most pet snake keepers can safely skip it, and how to cool a ball python or corn snake properly if you plan to breed.

articles.title: Is a Sugar Glider Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: Sugar gliders are illegal in California, Alaska, and Hawaii, and essentially unobtainable in Pennsylvania. Everywhere else they're generally legal, despite what several popular blog posts claim about Georgia, Massachusetts, and Wyoming.

articles.title: Is a Sulcata Tortoise Legal in Your State? Full Guide
articles.excerpt: No US state bans owning a sulcata tortoise. The real legal fact is a federal import ban dating to 2000, and the real practical problem is that these tortoises grow to about 200 pounds and overwhelm rescues nationwide.

articles.title: Tarantula Cost: Full Price Breakdown
articles.excerpt: Tarantulas are one of the most affordable exotic pets to keep, both upfront and over time, but a female can live 15 to 20 years or more, which makes the low sticker price a bit misleading if you only look at year one.

articles.title: Can You Handle a Tarantula Safely?
articles.excerpt: If you're hoping for a pet you can hold, a tarantula is probably the wrong choice. Handling carries real risk in both directions, a fall can be fatal to the spider, and its defenses can genuinely hurt you.

articles.title: Tarantula Health Issues: Signs & Causes
articles.excerpt: Veterinary care for tarantulas is genuinely limited, so prevention through correct husbandry does most of the heavy lifting, and knowing what's normal versus what's actually wrong matters more than knowing what to do once something's gone wrong.

articles.title: Tarantula Enclosure Setup Guide
articles.excerpt: Tarantula enclosures are refreshingly simple compared to most exotic pets, no complex lighting, no elaborate temperature gradients, but enclosure shape and substrate depth matter quite a bit.

articles.title: UVB Lighting: Why It Matters More Than You Think
articles.excerpt: Recent research has changed what we know about reptile UVB needs - including species once thought not to need it. Here's the updated picture.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Ball Python Facts
articles.excerpt: From heat-sensing pits to 7,000 color morphs, these 10 ball python facts explain why they're the most popular pet snake in the world.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Bearded Dragon Facts
articles.excerpt: From UV-sensing eyes to third eyelids, these 10 bearded dragon facts will surprise even experienced reptile owners and enthusiasts.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Betta Fish Facts
articles.excerpt: From breathing air to bubble nests, these 10 betta fish facts reveal just how much personality is packed into such a small tank.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Cockatoo Facts
articles.excerpt: From dancing to a beat to bending metal cage bars, these 10 cockatoo facts show why this parrot is as impressive as it is demanding.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Hermit Crab Facts
articles.excerpt: From decades-long lifespans to orderly shell-swapping queues, these 10 hermit crab facts go well beyond the beach-souvenir reputation.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Leopard Gecko Facts
articles.excerpt: From squeaking when startled to storing fat in their tails, these 10 leopard gecko facts go beyond the basics most keepers already know.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Praying Mantis Facts
articles.excerpt: From five eyes to bat-detecting ears, these 10 praying mantis facts go well beyond the classic 'prayer pose' most people already know.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Tarantula Facts
articles.excerpt: From blue blood to decades-long lifespans, these 10 tarantula facts reveal why they're far less scary and far more fascinating than their reputation suggests.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Axolotl Facts
articles.excerpt: Ten quick axolotl facts most people don't know - from limb regeneration and forever-young biology to why these smiling salamanders never leave the water.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Boa Constrictor Facts
articles.excerpt: Ten surprising boa constrictor facts - from heartbeat-syncing squeezes to live litters of 60 - and why they're the pet snake world's favorite.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Cuttlefish Facts
articles.excerpt: Cuttlefish can change their skin in milliseconds and have W-shaped pupils. Discover 10 remarkable facts about this intelligent cephalopod.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Golden Retriever Facts
articles.excerpt: Beyond their friendly smiles, golden retrievers have surprising quirks and hidden talents. Discover 10 facts about this iconic family dog.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Humpback Whale Facts
articles.excerpt: From haunting songs that travel thousands of miles to acrobatic breaches, these 10 humpback whale facts reveal the wonder of ocean life.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Octopus Facts
articles.excerpt: Think you know octopuses? These 10 facts reveal their three hearts, blue blood, and remarkable problem-solving intelligence.

articles.title: 10 Surprising Rabbit Facts
articles.excerpt: Ten surprising rabbit facts - from near-360-degree vision and teeth that never stop growing to binkies and why rabbits are smarter than they look.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Afternoon in the Grass (Part 6)
chronicles.excerpt: Mike sets up Dex's first outdoor sun pen, and the bearded dragon meets real wind, real grass, and one supremely unimpressed blue jay, in this funny, humbling short story about just how big the world turns out to be.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Day Everything Changed (Part 2)
chronicles.excerpt: Dex is back. Part 2 of the bearded dragon chronicles brings new trouble, warmer basking spots, and one lizard who refuses to be ignored.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Dragon Who Flew (Part 8)
chronicles.excerpt: Dex dozes on his basking rock and dreams he has sprouted wings, leaving the ground for the first time to see his whole small kingdom, terrarium, house, yard, and neighborhood, laid out in miniature below him, in this wondrous short story about flight, perspective, and home.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Pirate of the Sand Sea (Part 5)
chronicles.excerpt: A long soak sends Dex into a dream where he commands a ship across a golden sand sea, in this swashbuckling short story about rivalry, command, and the one line even a pirate king won't cross.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Rival Beyond the Glass (Part 3)
chronicles.excerpt: Dex faces his oldest mystery, the orange shape beyond the glass in this humorous short story about rivalry, territory, and the unexpected.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Sun, the Glass, and the Cricket That Got Away
chronicles.excerpt: Meet Dex: a bearded dragon with opinions, ambitions, and a cricket problem. This bearded dragon short story kicks off a charming, witty animal fiction series.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Twelfth Day (Part 4)
chronicles.excerpt: Dex has settled into an eleven-day ritual with the cat beyond the glass, until the twelfth day arrives and the windowsill stays empty, in this quietly funny short story about routine, absence, and what a king starts counting on.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: The Waiting Room (Part 9)
chronicles.excerpt: Dex is carried off in his travel tub for a checkup and lands in a vet's waiting room ruled by a loudly opinionated parrot and a magnificently put-upon chinchilla, in this ensemble comedy short story about dignity, chaos, and discovering the world is bigger than the glass.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Dex the Bearded Dragon: What the Glass Was For (Part 7)
chronicles.excerpt: When a window is left open a crack on a warm afternoon, Dex and Whiskey come closer to real contact than four years of glass-pressed friendship ever allowed, and Dex learns what the boundary between them was truly protecting all along.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: First Flight (Part 4)
chronicles.excerpt: Dozing in his hutch on a hot afternoon, Otis dreams that he can fly, rising above the only garden he's ever known to see its whole shape, and the neighborhood beyond it, for the first time.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Bark, the Gap, and the Matter of Proportion (Part 3)
chronicles.excerpt: Otis spends eleven days building a rival worthy of his fence line, then finds the gap in the boards and discovers the truth is a lot smaller than the legend he invented.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Day He Decided the Garden Belonged to Him
chronicles.excerpt: Follow along in this Otis the bunny story — a charming short fiction about one determined rabbit navigating the big drama hiding in very small moments.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Garden, the Squirrel, and the Matter of Trespassing (Part 2)
chronicles.excerpt: A squirrel raids Otis's garden and gets away clean, leaving him to face an uncomfortable truth: there might be a whole world beyond his fence he's never once considered.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Great Warren Expedition (Part 8)
chronicles.excerpt: Otis falls asleep under the hydrangea and wakes up leading a grand expedition across his own garden turned continent, over the Sea of the Undrained and up Mount Compostia, before opening his eyes to find how small his kingdom really is.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Guest in the House (Part 6)
chronicles.excerpt: When Mike agrees to pet-sit a friend's guinea pig, Otis has to share his prized sunny corner with a chattier, far more anxious houseguest than himself. It takes a piece of romaine lettuce to settle what territorial pacing alone could not.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Night of the Owl (Part 7)
chronicles.excerpt: Otis stays out past his usual hour and finally meets a danger unlike the dog, the squirrel, or the cottontail before it: a real owl, low and silent over the grass, teaching him in under two seconds that the sky is not entirely his.

chronicles.title: Chronicles of Otis the Bunny: The Trip to Somewhere New (Part 5)
chronicles.excerpt: Mike drives Otis to a friend's backyard farm for the first time, where he meets a hen with just as much confidence about her turf as he has about his, and learns the world beyond his fence is bigger, and considerably more crowded with self-important small animals, than he thought.
